methods.description: Process for collecting and preserving epiphytic diatom samples from host plants for qualitative and quantitative analysis.

steps.description: Samples of epiphytic diatoms were collected monthly for two seasons (Autumn, 2018 to Winter, 2019) from three sites.
steps.searchable_summary: collect epiphytic diatom samples monthly from specified sites

steps.description: The samples were put in polyethylene containers with a small amount of water environment and 5-10ml of 4% formalin were added to solution in the field until arrival to laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: preserve diatom samples with formalin in polyethylene containers

methods.description: Procedure for preparing diatom samples for qualitative analysis by cutting host plants and preserving samples with Lugol solution.

steps.description: Weight 10g of host plants were cut into small parts 2-3 cm and shaken with 50-100ml of environment water then scrapped surface plant by smooth brusher or the blade isn't sharp.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare host plant samples by cutting and shaking with water

steps.description: Samples were preserved in plastic containers with 1ml of Lugol solution.
steps.searchable_summary: preserve plant samples with Lugol solution

methods.description: Procedure for separating diatom cells from host plants and preparing them for quantitative analysis.

steps.description: Separate diatom cells from host plants (P. australis, T. domengensis), then put the sample of diatom cells in cylinders of (100ml) for 10-15 days with 1ml of Lugol solution for precipitation.
steps.searchable_summary: separate diatom cells and precipitate with Lugol solution

steps.description: Keep precipitating (20-30ml) in containers with added drops of Lugol solution.
steps.searchable_summary: store precipitated diatom cells with Lugol solution

methods.description: Process for identifying epiphytic diatoms using key references and light microscopy.

steps.description: Prepared of permanent of epiphytic diatom for identification by the light microscope 100x.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare diatom samples for light microscopy

steps.description: Identify epiphytic diatoms depended on key references.
